title: The CPython GIL serializes bytecodes, not whole statements, so += is not atomic
rule: The CPython GIL guarantees one bytecode runs without interleaving, NOT that a whole statement is indivisible: the interpreter can switch threads between the load and the store of x += 1, counter = counter + 1, or any check-then-act, so they race across threads and lose updates. Guard shared mutable state with a Lock or a queue; never assume a Python statement is atomic because of the GIL.
triggers: a threaded counter loses increments under the GIL
x += 1 is not atomic across Python threads
race condition in Python threads despite the GIL
counter = counter + 1 interleaves between two threads
